hops.text: weather | General | To begin to keep myself as warm as I can.
work | General | Up by three, and going by four on my way to London; but the day proves very cold, so that having put on
work | Work | Up, and it being a brave morning, with a gally to Woolwich, and there both at the Ropeyarde and the other
work | General | Having taken our leaves of Sir W. Batten and my Lady, who are gone this morning to keep their Whitsunti
work | General | This morning Sir W. Batten with Colonel Birch to Deptford, to pay off two ships. Sir W. Penn and I stay
work | General | . Up, and with Sir W. Batten and [Sir] W. Penn to Whitehall, and there to Sir W. Coventry’s chamber, an
work | General | Up, and with Sir W. Batten and Sir W. Penn to Whitehall; but there finding the Duke gone to his lodging
